steps.title: Identify the volume formula for a cylinder
steps.content: The volume of a right circular cylinder equals the base area times the height. We are given that the volume is \$377$ cubic centimeters and the base area is \$13$ square centimeters.

steps.title: Substitute and solve for height
steps.content: Substitute the known values into the formula: $$377 = 13h$$. Divide both sides by 13: $$h = \frac{377}{13} = 29$$. The height of the cylinder is 29 centimeters.
